text.title: Mob Programming Core Rules
text.summary: 5 rules for a working mob session with AI in the room.

rule.statement: Cap is 4 humans + 1 agent. Beyond this, attention disperses and the mob becomes a meeting.

rationale.source: Hill & Bird — Mob Programming Guide; internal session retros

rule.rationale: Empirical: groups >5 voices lose convergence; one engaged voice typically goes silent.

rule.statement: Driver rotates every 5-15 minutes; rotation logged.
rule.rationale: Without rotation the same person types; others disengage.

rule.statement: For an idea to go from your head to the screen it MUST go through someone else's hands.
rule.rationale: Forces verbalisation, exposes assumptions, distributes ownership.

rule.statement: Agent may be transcriber, researcher, or snippet-retriever; agent is NEVER the primary navigator.
rule.rationale: Primary navigator owns direction; an agent without context will steer wrong.

rule.statement: One human facilitator is named before the session starts; their job is rotation + agenda + decision capture.
rule.rationale: Unnamed facilitator role = no facilitator; conversations drift.
